esp8266.title: ESP8266 (ESP8266EX)
notes: ESP8266: GPIO0, GPIO2, and GPIO15 are strapping pins; use them only if the external circuit preserves the required boot level.
GPIO6-11 are normally reserved for the external SPI flash interface and should not be used as GPIO.
GPIO4, GPIO5, GPIO12, GPIO13, and GPIO14 are general-purpose pins with no boot strap restrictions.
UART0 (GPIO1 TX, GPIO3 RX) is used for programming and logs. Reusing these pins can disrupt flashing or serial communication.
GPIO16 is a special RTC/deep-sleep wake pin with limited GPIO features; A0/ADC0 is analog-input-only.

rows.warning: Strapping (BOOT0) - must be HIGH at boot (LOW forces flash mode). After boot it can be used as a normal input. ESPHome warns about strapping pins.
UART0 TX outputs boot logs. Default state is HIGH at boot; boot can fail if forced LOW. Avoid attaching devices that react to boot pulses.
Strapping - must be HIGH at boot; LOW prevents boot. Often connected to on-board LED. ESPHome warns about strapping pins.
UART0 RX is used for serial communication. Pull-up at boot. It can be driven as an output in software*, but this is nonstandard; keep for serial/programming.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. No boot restrictions. Safe for relays and peripherals.
General-purpose GPIO. Often used as I2C SCL. Safe at boot.

rows.error: Reserved (Flash) - connected to external SPI flash. Do not use as GPIO. ESPHome blocks these pins.
Reserved (Flash) - connected to external SPI flash. Do not use as GPIO. ESPHome blocks these pins.
Reserved (Flash) - connected to external SPI flash. Do not use as GPIO. ESPHome blocks these pins.
Reserved (Flash) - connected to external SPI flash. Do not use as GPIO. ESPHome blocks these pins.
Reserved (Flash) - connected to external SPI flash. Do not use as GPIO. ESPHome blocks these pins.
Reserved (Flash) - connected to external SPI flash. Do not use as GPIO. ESPHome blocks these pins.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. Often SPI MISO. No boot restrictions.
General-purpose GPIO. Often SPI MOSI. No boot restrictions.
General-purpose GPIO. Often SPI SCLK. Generates a short PWM pulse at reset.

rows.warning: Strapping - must be LOW at boot. Often used as SPI CS. ESPHome warns about strapping pins.
Special GPIO16 (D0). No interrupts, PWM, or I2C. Default HIGH at boot. Used for deep sleep wake (connects to RESET).
Analog input only. 0-1V on bare chip, up to 3.3V on modules with divider.

esp32.title: ESP32 (original ESP32, WROOM-32)
notes: ESP32: GPIO0, GPIO2, GPIO5, GPIO12, and GPIO15 are strapping pins sampled during reset; external circuitry must not force an invalid boot level.
GPIO6-11 are normally connected to SPI flash and should not be used. On modules with PSRAM, GPIO16-17 can also be unavailable; verify the exact module.
GPIO34-39 are input-only and do not have software-enabled internal pull-up/pull-down resistors.
GPIO1 (TXD0) and GPIO3 (RXD0) are used for UART0 programming/logs. Reuse is possible, but attached circuitry must tolerate boot logs and flashing.
I2C and SPI signals can usually be remapped through the GPIO matrix; default peripheral labels alone are not a reason to mark a pin as caution.

rows.warning: Strapping - GPIO0 selects boot mode. Must be HIGH at boot (LOW forces flash). ESPHome warns about strapping pins.
UART0 TX outputs boot logs (default HIGH at boot). If forced LOW during reset, boot can fail. Avoid devices that pull it down.
Strapping - must be LOW/FLOAT at boot. Pulling HIGH prevents boot. ESPHome warns about strapping pins.
UART0 RX is used for programming/logs. Default pull-up at boot. Can be output in software*, but keep for UART0 when possible.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. No strapping function. Safe at boot. Supports ADC2_CH0 and touch T0.

rows.warning: Strapping - must be HIGH at boot. Emits PWM during reset. Default VSPI CS0. ESPHome warns about strapping pins.

rows.error: Reserved (SPI Flash) - connected to internal flash. Do not use. ESPHome blocks these pins.
Reserved (SPI Flash) - connected to internal flash. Do not use. ESPHome blocks these pins.
Reserved (SPI Flash) - connected to internal flash. Do not use. ESPHome blocks these pins.
Reserved (SPI Flash) - connected to internal flash. Do not use. ESPHome blocks these pins.
Reserved (SPI Flash) - connected to internal flash. Do not use. ESPHome blocks these pins.
Reserved (SPI Flash) - connected to internal flash. Do not use. ESPHome blocks these pins.

rows.warning: Strapping - must be LOW at boot to keep flash at 3.3V. HIGH can force 1.8V and break boot. ESPHome warns about strapping pins.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO (ADC2_CH4, Touch T4). No boot restrictions.
General-purpose GPIO (ADC2_CH6, Touch T6). Emits PWM during reset.

rows.warning: Strapping - must be HIGH at boot. Emits PWM during reset. ESPHome warns about strapping pins.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO (ADC2_CH8). UART2 RX on some boards.
General-purpose GPIO (ADC2_CH9). UART2 TX on some boards.
General-purpose GPIO. Default I2C SDA on many dev kits.
General-purpose GPIO. Default I2C SCL on many dev kits.

rows.warning: Input-only GPIO34 (ADC1_CH6, RTC_GPIO4).
Input-only GPIO35 (ADC1_CH7, RTC_GPIO5).
Input-only GPIO36 (ADC1_CH0, RTC_GPIO0).
Input-only GPIO37 (ADC1_CH1). Not broken out on some modules.
Input-only GPIO38 (ADC1_CH2). Not broken out on some modules.
Input-only GPIO39 (ADC1_CH3, RTC_GPIO3).

esp32-s2.title: ESP32-S2 (ESP32-S2-SOLO)
notes: ESP32-S2: GPIO0, GPIO45, and GPIO46 are strapping pins sampled during reset; external circuitry must preserve the required boot levels.
GPIO46 is input-only and has a fixed pull-down. GPIO26-32 are usually used for SPI flash/PSRAM and are not recommended for GPIO.
GPIO19 and GPIO20 are the native USB D-/D+ pins. Use them as GPIO only when native USB is not needed.
GPIO39-42 are JTAG pins; they can be used as GPIO if JTAG debugging is not used.
ADC, touch, RTC, I2C, and SPI alternate functions do not by themselves make a pin caution.

rows.warning: Strapping - selects boot mode. Must be HIGH at boot (LOW forces flash).

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. On DevKitC-1 controls built-in addressable RGB LED.

rows.warning: Native USB D- pin. Use as GPIO only when native USB is not required.
Native USB D+ pin. Use as GPIO only when native USB is not required.

rows.error: Reserved (Flash) - GPIO26-32 connected to internal flash/PSRAM and not available.
Reserved (Flash).
Reserved (Flash).
Reserved (Flash).
Reserved (Flash).
Reserved (Flash).
Reserved (Flash).

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. Also JTAG TCK; using it as GPIO prevents use of that JTAG signal.
General-purpose GPIO. Also JTAG TMS; using it as GPIO prevents use of that JTAG signal.
General-purpose GPIO. Also JTAG TDI; using it as GPIO prevents use of that JTAG signal.
General-purpose GPIO. Also JTAG TDO; using it as GPIO prevents use of that JTAG signal.

rows.warning: Strapping - GPIO45 selects VDD_SDIO voltage. Ensure correct default state; invalid levels can break flash.
Strapping and input-only GPIO46. Fixed pull-down; cannot be used as an output.

esp32-s3.title: ESP32-S3 (ESP32-S3-WROOM)
notes: ESP32-S3: GPIO0, GPIO3, GPIO45, and GPIO46 are strapping pins sampled during reset; GPIO45 is especially sensitive because it affects VDD_SPI/flash-voltage selection.
GPIO22-25 are not valid GPIO numbers on ESP32-S3. GPIO26-32 are usually used for SPI flash/PSRAM and are not recommended for GPIO.
GPIO19 and GPIO20 are used by USB-Serial/JTAG by default; using them as GPIO disables that USB-JTAG function.
GPIO33-37 may be connected to Octal flash/PSRAM on ESP32-S3R8/R8V-class modules, so verify the exact module before using them.
GPIO43 and GPIO44 are UART0 pins used for flashing/logs on many boards. ADC/touch alternate functions alone are not a warning condition.

rows.warning: Strapping - selects boot mode. Pull-up; must be HIGH at boot.
Strapping - selects JTAG source. Keep default pull-down at boot.
USB-Serial/JTAG D- pin by default. Use as GPIO only when USB-Serial/JTAG is not required.
USB-Serial/JTAG D+ pin by default. Use as GPIO only when USB-Serial/JTAG is not required.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. Available on ESP32-S3-WROOM modules.

rows.error: Not a valid GPIO on ESP32-S3 (GPIO range skips GPIO22-GPIO25).
Not a valid GPIO on ESP32-S3 (GPIO range skips GPIO22-GPIO25).
Not a valid GPIO on ESP32-S3 (GPIO range skips GPIO22-GPIO25).
Not a valid GPIO on ESP32-S3 (GPIO range skips GPIO22-GPIO25).
Reserved (Flash) - GPIO26-32 connected to flash/PSRAM and not available.
Reserved (Flash/PSRAM).
Reserved (Flash/PSRAM).
Reserved (Flash/PSRAM).
Reserved (Flash/PSRAM).
Reserved (Flash/PSRAM).
Reserved (Flash/PSRAM).

rows.warning: General-purpose GPIO on some ESP32-S3 variants; may be reserved for Octal flash/PSRAM on S3R8/R8V-class modules. Verify module variant.
General-purpose GPIO on some ESP32-S3 variants; may be reserved for Octal flash/PSRAM on S3R8/R8V-class modules. Verify module variant.
General-purpose GPIO on some ESP32-S3 variants; may be reserved for Octal flash/PSRAM on S3R8/R8V-class modules. Verify module variant.
General-purpose GPIO on some ESP32-S3 variants; may be reserved for Octal flash/PSRAM on S3R8/R8V-class modules. Verify module variant.
General-purpose GPIO on some ESP32-S3 variants; may be reserved for Octal flash/PSRAM on S3R8/R8V-class modules. Verify module variant.
UART0 RX connected to USB-UART. Keep for programming.
UART0 TX connected to USB-UART. Emits boot logs.
Strapping pin for VDD_SPI/flash-voltage selection. Keep the required default level at reset; avoid external pulls that change boot configuration.
Strapping - participates in boot mode selection and ROM log control. Avoid pull-ups to VCC.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. On DevKitC controls RGB LED blue channel.

esp32-c2.title: ESP32-C2 (ESP8684)
notes: ESP32-C2/ESP8684: GPIO8 and GPIO9 are strapping pins and must not be forced to an invalid level during reset.
For SiP-flash variants, GPIO11-17 are dedicated to flash; GPIO0-7, GPIO10, and GPIO18-20 are normal GPIO unless board wiring says otherwise.
GPIO19 and GPIO20 are commonly used for UART0 programming/logs; reuse only if flashing/logging is not affected.
ADC/RTC alternate functions do not by themselves make a pin caution.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. On DevKitC-02 drives red LED.
General-purpose GPIO. On DevKit drives green LED.
General-purpose GPIO. Recommended as I2C SDA.
General-purpose GPIO. Recommended as I2C SCL.
General-purpose GPIO. ADC2 analog reads may conflict with Wi-Fi; digital IO is fine.

rows.warning: Strapping - affects boot mode. Do not pull LOW during reset. On DevKit drives blue LED.
Strapping - boot/log strap. Internal pull-up. Avoid forcing LOW at boot.
UART0 RX for programming. Keep free when flashing.
UART0 TX for boot logs and programming. Avoid using for peripherals.

esp32-c3.title: ESP32-C3 (ESP32-C3-WROOM-02)
notes: ESP32-C3: GPIO2, GPIO8, and GPIO9 are strapping pins sampled during reset.
GPIO12-17 are normally used for SPI flash/PSRAM and are not recommended for GPIO.
GPIO18 and GPIO19 are used by USB-Serial/JTAG by default; using them as GPIO disables that function.
GPIO20 and GPIO21 are commonly UART0 RX/TX pins for programming/logs.
GPIO5 is ADC2; analog reads can conflict with Wi-Fi, but digital GPIO use is normal.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. Also RTC 32k_XP when using external 32kHz oscillator.
General-purpose GPIO. Also RTC 32k_XN when using external 32kHz oscillator.

rows.warning: Strapping - participates in boot mode selection. Avoid pull-up at reset.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. ADC2 analog reads may conflict with Wi-Fi; digital IO is fine.
General-purpose GPIO. Commonly used as SPI SCLK on some boards.
General-purpose GPIO. Commonly used as SPI MOSI on some boards.

rows.warning: Strapping - controls ROM log output and boot mode. Often drives blue LED.
Strapping - bootloader strap. Tied to BOOT button on dev kits.

rows.note: General-purpose GPIO. Default SPI CS1 on some boards.

rows.error: Not available as GPIO.
Not available as GPIO.
Not available as GPIO.
Not available as GPIO.
Not available as GPIO.
Not available as GPIO.
Not available as GPIO.

rows.warning: USB-Serial/JTAG default D- (USB_JTAG_TMS). Keep free if using USB-JTAG.
USB-Serial/JTAG default D+ (USB_JTAG_TCK). Keep free if using USB-JTAG.
UART0 RX for programming/logs. Avoid strong external drive during flashing.
UART0 TX outputs boot logs and is used during flashing. Avoid connecting sensitive peripherals.

esp32-c5.title: ESP32-C5 (ESP32-C5-WROOM-1)
notes: ESP32-C5: GPIO2, GPIO7, GPIO25, GPIO27, and GPIO28 are strapping pins in current ESP-IDF documentation.
GPIO16-22 are normally used for SPI flash/PSRAM and are not recommended for GPIO.
GPIO13 and GPIO14 are used by USB-JTAG by default; using them as GPIO disables USB-JTAG.
GPIO11 and GPIO12 may be used as UART0 on boards that expose UART flashing/logs.
ESP32-C5 has GPIO0-GPIO28 only; GPIO29 and higher are not valid GPIOs for this chip.

rows.warning: Strapping pin sampled during reset. Keep the required default boot level.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. Keep the required default boot level.
UART0 TX on some boards. Use as GPIO only if UART flashing/logging is not required.
UART0 RX on some boards. Use as GPIO only if UART flashing/logging is not required.
USB-JTAG D- pin by default. Use as GPIO only when USB-JTAG is not required.
USB-JTAG D+ pin by default. Use as GPIO only when USB-JTAG is not required.

esp32-c6.title: ESP32-C6 (ESP32-C6-WROOM-1)
notes: ESP32-C6: GPIO4, GPIO5, GPIO8, GPIO9, and GPIO15 are strapping pins sampled during reset.
GPIO12 and GPIO13 are used by USB-JTAG by default; using them as GPIO disables USB-JTAG.
GPIO24-30 are normally used for SPI flash/PSRAM and are not recommended for GPIO.
ESP32-C6 has GPIO0-GPIO30 only; GPIO34-GPIO38 are not valid GPIOs for this chip.
GPIO2, GPIO3, GPIO7, GPIO16-GPIO23 are normal GPIOs unless board wiring reserves them.

rows.warning: Strapping pin sampled during reset. Keep the required default boot level.
Strapping pin sampled during reset. Keep the required default boot level.

rows.error: SPI0/1 flash/PSRAM interface on ESP32-C6 modules. Do not use unless board documentation explicitly confirms it is free.

esp32-h2.title: ESP32-H2 (ESP32-H2-MINI-1)
notes: ESP32-H2: GPIO2, GPIO3, GPIO8, GPIO9, and GPIO25 are strapping pins sampled during reset.
For SiP-flash variants, GPIO15-21 are dedicated to SPI flash, and GPIO6-7 are not led out.
GPIO26 and GPIO27 are used by USB-Serial-JTAG by default; using them as GPIO disables that function.
GPIO0-5, GPIO8-14, and GPIO22-27 are the user-available ranges on SiP-flash variants, subject to strapping and USB/JTAG caveats.
Analog comparator or RTC alternate functions do not by themselves make a pin caution.

Strapping pin sampled during reset. Keep the required default boot level.

rows.error: Not led out on ESP32-H2 SiP-flash variants; avoid unless your exact chip/package exposes it.

GPIO0-GPIO54 are valid chip GPIOs; actual availability still depends on package and board/module routing.
Touch, ADC, LP-GPIO, I2C, SPI, or other alternate functions do not by themselves make a pin caution.
